steps.title: Interpret the exponent
steps.content:
The base $1.03$ means one $3\%$ increase happens each time the exponent increases by $1$. In one year, the exponent increases by
$$
\frac{8}{6}=\frac{4}{3}
$$
so there are $\frac{4}{3}$ growth periods per year.

steps.title: Convert periods to months
steps.content: If each growth period is $n$ months, then one year has $\frac{12}{n}$ periods: $$ \frac{12}{n}=\frac{4}{3} $$ $$ n=12\div\frac{4}{3}=9 $$ The answer is A.
